zh.lifeSummary: 當John Barentyne於1380年之後出生在英國艾可奧加時，他的父親Nicolas Barrington8648歲，母親Margaret Clovill8655歲。他於1398年與Alice Battell結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1426年在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國艾可雷利。

nameMeanings.name: Barentyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ballentine
meanings: Scottish: habitational name, probably from the lands of Bellenden, now Bellendean, on Ale Water west of Roberton in Roxburghshire. John Ballantyne, archdeacon of Moray 1533–38, was also recorded as John Ballentyne or Bellenden. It has been suggested that the placename is from Gaelic baile an deadhain ‘the dean's farm’, which leaves open the possibility of more than one geographical origin (there is also a place called Bellenden in Selkirk); the surname is persistently associated with Falkirk and Edinburgh as well as Roxburghshire. The origin of the variation between -d- and -t-, if genuine, is unknown.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John Barentyne was born after 1380, in Ongar, Essex, England, his father, Nicolas Barrington, was 8648 and his mother, Margaret Clovill, was 8655. He married Alice Battell in 1398. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died about 1426, in his hometown, and was buried in Rayleigh, Essex, England.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Barentyne родился после 1380 г. в местности онгар, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Nicolas Barrington было 8648 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Clovill, 8655. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Battell в 1398 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер около 1426 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности реилиг, эссекс, Англия.
